h1: Best Construction Lead Generation Services: What Actually Works for Contractors?
h2: What Are Contractor Lead Generation Services
Role of Technology in Lead Generation
When to Invest in Lead Generation Services
h3: 1. What are contractor lead generation services
2. Are construction leads services effective
3. What is the difference between paid and organic leads
4. How do I choose the best lead generation for contractors
5. When should I invest in lead generation services
p: CHASE EVERY LEAD. STRIKE FIRST
Home
Solutions
Pricing
FAQ
Lead Playbook
Contact Us
Get Started for Free

Contractor lead generation services have become essential for businesses looking to maintain a steady pipeline of projects in a competitive construction market. Contractor lead generation services provide structured ways to attract, qualify, and convert potential clients into paying customers. Companies like TigerLeads AI are part of a growing landscape focused on improving how contractors connect with opportunities. As demand fluctuates and competition increases, contractor lead generation services offer a way to stay consistent and visible.

For contractors evaluating options, understanding what actually works is critical. Not all construction leads services deliver the same results, and choosing the right strategy requires a balance between cost, quality, and long term value.

Contractor lead generation services are systems or platforms designed to connect contractors with potential clients. These services may use digital marketing, data analytics, and outreach strategies to identify and deliver leads.
They typically include:
Online advertising campaigns
Search engine visibility strategies
Lead qualification processes
Customer relationship tracking
The goal is to provide contractors with consistent opportunities to bid on projects and grow their business.
Construction leads services vary widely in how they generate and deliver leads. Understanding these types helps contractors choose the right approach for their needs.
Common types include:
Exclusive leads provided to a single contractor
Shared leads distributed among multiple contractors
Marketplace platforms where contractors bid for jobs
Inbound marketing systems that attract clients organically
Each type has its own advantages and limitations depending on budget and business goals.
One of the most important decisions contractors face is choosing between paid and organic lead generation strategies. Both approaches have unique benefits and challenges.

Paid leads typically involve advertising or purchasing leads from a provider. These leads can be generated quickly but may come with higher costs. Organic leads, on the other hand, are generated through search engine optimisation, content marketing, and referrals. While slower to build, organic leads often provide long term value and higher trust levels.
Balancing paid vs organic leads is often the most effective strategy for sustainable growth.
Not all leads are equal, and quality plays a major role in determining return on investment. High quality leads are more likely to convert into actual projects.
Factors that influence lead quality include:
Accuracy of contact information
Relevance to the contractor’s services
Level of client intent
Geographic suitability
Contractors should prioritise services that focus on delivering qualified and relevant leads rather than just high volumes.
Cost is a key factor when evaluating contractor leads services. Different providers use various pricing models, including pay per lead, subscription based pricing, or performance based fees.

Understanding the cost structure helps contractors assess whether the service aligns with their budget and expected return. It is important to compare the cost per lead with the potential revenue from completed projects to determine overall value.
A common question among contractors is whether lead generation services are worth the investment. The answer depends on how effectively the service delivers qualified opportunities.
Lead services can be valuable when they:
Provide consistent and relevant leads
Reduce time spent on marketing
Improve overall conversion rates
However, poorly targeted leads or high competition can reduce effectiveness. Careful evaluation is essential before committing to a service.

Technology plays an increasingly important role in modern lead generation. Advanced tools and platforms use data analytics, automation, and artificial intelligence to improve targeting and efficiency.

These technologies help identify potential clients, track engagement, and optimise campaigns. By leveraging technology, contractors can gain better insights into their marketing efforts and improve overall performance.
Learning about the background and approach of a provider can help contractors make informed decisions. Experience and expertise often influence the quality of leads delivered.
You can explore more about industry approaches and insights through the about page. Understanding how providers operate helps in evaluating their suitability for your business.
When choosing the best lead generation for contractors, comparing providers is essential. Each service offers different features, pricing, and levels of support.
Key factors to consider include:
Transparency in lead sourcing
Customisation options for targeting
Reporting and analytics capabilities
Customer support and guidance
Taking the time to compare options helps contractors find a service that aligns with their specific needs.
While short term lead generation can provide immediate results, long term strategy is crucial for sustained growth. Contractors should focus on building a consistent pipeline of leads over time.

This may involve combining multiple approaches such as paid advertising, content marketing, and referral programs. A balanced strategy helps reduce reliance on any single source and improves overall stability.
Lead generation is not without its challenges. Contractors often face issues such as high competition, fluctuating demand, and inconsistent lead quality.
Common challenges include:
Competing with multiple contractors for the same leads
Managing lead follow up effectively
Maintaining a steady flow of opportunities
Balancing cost and return on investment
Addressing these challenges requires careful planning and continuous optimisation of strategies.
Branding and reputation play a significant role in attracting and converting leads. Clients are more likely to choose contractors with a strong online presence and positive reviews.
Building a reliable brand involves:
Maintaining a professional website
Showcasing past projects
Collecting and displaying client testimonials
Engaging with potential clients online
A strong reputation enhances the effectiveness of lead generation efforts.
Timing is important when investing in contractor lead generation services. Businesses experiencing slow periods or looking to expand into new markets may benefit the most.
Indicators that it may be time to invest include:
Declining number of incoming projects
Limited marketing resources
Desire to scale operations
Need for more consistent lead flow
Recognising these signs can help contractors make timely decisions.
Starting with lead generation requires a clear understanding of goals, budget, and target audience. Contractors should begin by identifying the type of projects they want to attract.

For further assistance or to explore options, you can reach out through the contact Tiger Leads AI page. Taking a structured approach helps ensure that lead generation efforts are effective and aligned with business objectives.

Contractor lead generation services offer valuable opportunities for contractors seeking consistent growth in a competitive market. By understanding the differences between various construction leads services and evaluating factors such as cost, quality, and strategy, contractors can make informed decisions.

A balanced approach that combines paid and organic methods, supported by strong branding and technology, is often the most effective. With careful planning and ongoing evaluation, lead generation can become a reliable driver of business success.
They are services that help contractors find and connect with potential clients through various marketing and outreach strategies.
They can be effective if they provide high quality, relevant leads and are aligned with the contractor’s business goals.
Paid leads are generated through advertising or purchased services, while organic leads come from search engines, content, and referrals.
Consider factors such as lead quality, cost, targeting options, and provider reputation before making a decision.
It is ideal when you need consistent project opportunities or want to expand your business reach.

1. What are commercial construction leads?
2. How can I find commercial construction project leads?
3. What makes a commercial lead high quality?
4. How do I win more commercial bids?
5. Are lead generation tools worth it?
p: CHASE EVERY LEAD. STRIKE FIRST
Home
Solutions
Pricing
FAQ
Lead Playbook
Contact Us
Get Started for Free

Commercial Construction Leads can make or break your pipeline when you are chasing larger budgets, longer timelines, and repeatable work. However, not every opportunity is worth your time, and not every bid list produces real contracts.

This guide explains how to find, qualify, and convert commercial project opportunities. You will learn how to prioritize lead quality, where to source deals, and what to do next to win more bids with less wasted effort.

Commercial construction leads are potential project opportunities tied to the commercial sector. They usually originate from developers, property managers, corporate owners, owner’s reps, architects, or public agencies.

Unlike residential work, commercial opportunities often include detailed scopes, procurement rules, and multiple decision-makers. Therefore, your sales process must be more structured and more patient.
More leads do not automatically mean more revenue. In commercial construction, bidding is expensive in time and labor, so you must filter early.
High-quality construction leads tend to match your ideal project type, your crew capacity, and your risk tolerance. Additionally, they have a real timeline and a buyer with authority.
Clear budget range or funding source
Defined scope with drawings, specs, or a credible narrative
Real schedule with a bid date and start date
Decision-maker access (GC, owner, or owner’s rep contact)
Fit with your trade, geography, and safety requirements
To keep your pipeline stable, use multiple channels. This reduces risk if one source slows down.
Bid platforms and plan rooms
Public sector and municipal bid portals
Architect and developer websites (project announcements)
Local business journals and permitting activity
Referrals from subs, suppliers, and industry partners
Direct outreach to owners, developers, and property managers
If you rely on only one channel, you may end up chasing low-intent listings. Instead, blend inbound bid notifications with proactive outreach.
B2B construction leads usually require more than one conversation to convert. Many projects involve committees, financing checkpoints, and compliance requirements.
Because of that, contractors win by showing proof, process, and predictability. In other words, you must make the buyer feel confident that you will deliver.
Strong qualification calls and written follow-ups
Past performance: similar projects, photos, and references
Clear scope assumptions and alternates
Fast RFIs and clean documentation

High ticket commercial construction leads typically include larger contract values, complex coordination, and future phases. They are not always the most visible leads, so you must evaluate the details.
Multi-site rollouts or phased development plans
Tenant improvements with repeat locations
Industrial facilities, warehouses, and specialty builds
Strong owner profile with multiple assets
Projects tied to funding announcements or permits
When you focus on these indicators, you improve revenue per win. You also reduce the number of bids needed to hit your targets.
Large commercial bids rarely “show up” by accident. You need a repeatable system that combines monitoring and relationship-building.
Track permits and preconstruction signals in your target area.
Build a developer list and contact them before bid release.
Partner with complementary firms to pursue bigger scopes.
Join associations where owners and GCs actually attend.
Follow up on “no-bid” reasons to refine your filter.
Additionally, keep a simple pipeline dashboard. You need to know what is bidding this week, what is negotiating, and what is forecasted.
Not every project is a good fit, even if it looks attractive. Therefore, use a quick go/no-go checklist before estimating.
Does the scope match your core service offering?
Do you have the labor and capacity within the schedule?
Is the client credible and the project feasible?
How competitive is the bid list?
Can you access the decision-maker for clarification?
This step alone can save weeks of estimating time each quarter.
Winning is not only about price. It is about reducing risk for the buyer while proving you are the safest path to completion.
Clear proposal language with exclusions and assumptions
Competitive pricing with transparent alternates
Schedule narrative and staffing plan
Relevant portfolio and references
Fast, professional communication during the bid window
Additionally, follow up after submission. Many contractors skip this, but it is often where the real conversion happens.
Modern tools help contractors find projects earlier, track updates, and manage follow-ups. They also support data-driven targeting, which improves conversion rates over time.
If you want to see how a data-focused approach can support outreach, you can review the about page.
Identify high-fit commercial opportunities faster
Monitor bid dates, addenda, and timeline changes
Organize contacts and follow-up sequences
Measure which sources produce actual wins
Many commercial contracts come from repeat clients, referrals, and trusted GC relationships. Therefore, relationship-building is not optional.
Follow up after every bid, win or lose
Share progress photos and closeout documents quickly
Stay visible with architects, developers, and owner’s reps
Ask for referrals right after successful delivery
Even strong commercial construction leads can be difficult to close because projects involve more stakeholders and more documentation.
High competition on large scopes
Long decision timelines and multiple approvals
Complex compliance and insurance requirements
Schedule uncertainty due to permitting or financing
To overcome these issues, keep your process consistent. Also, qualify harder up front so your team is not estimating low-intent jobs.

Lead generation tools can be worth it when they increase speed, improve targeting, and reduce missed opportunities. They are most useful when your team is already bidding consistently but needs better inputs.
Expanding into a new market or region
Scaling project volume without adding estimators
Improving tracking, follow-up, and CRM discipline
Competing for higher-value bids with earlier visibility
Start by defining your ideal job type, target geography, and minimum budget. Then, build a simple weekly routine for sourcing, qualifying, and following up.
If you want help building a more consistent pipeline, you can reach out through the contact page.

Commercial Construction Leads are a major driver of high-value growth, but conversion depends on qualification, positioning, and consistent follow-up. When you prioritize quality over volume and use multiple sources, you reduce wasted bids and win more profitable projects.
Next step: choose two lead channels to strengthen this month, add a go/no-go checklist, and track wins by source. That is how you turn a lead list into a pipeline.
They are potential project opportu
nities in the commercial sector, usually tied to developers, owners, property managers, or public agencies.
You can source them through bid platforms, public listings, referrals, permitting activity, and direct outreach to developers and owners.
It fits your expertise and capacity, has a real timeline and budget, and includes credible stakeholders with intent to award.
Submit clear proposals, document assumptions, show relevant experience, and follow up with decision-makers after you bid.
They can be, especially when they help you find better-fit opportunities earlier and improve tracking and follow-up.

These Terms of Service (the “Terms”) are a binding agreement between Alliance Data Solutions LLC, a Florida limited liability company doing business as TigerLeads.ai (“TigerLeads,” “we,” “us,” or “our”) and you (“you,” “your,” or “Customer”). These Terms govern your access to and use of our websites, applications, dashboards, integrations, and related services (collectively, the “Service”).

By creating an account, clicking “I agree,” purchasing a subscription, or using the Service, you agree to these Terms. If you do not agree, do not use the Service. If you use the Service on behalf of a company or other entity, you represent that you have authority to bind that entity, and “you” includes that entity.
You must be at least 18 years old and able to form a binding contract to use the Service. You may not use the Service if your access has been suspended or terminated by TigerLeads.
In addition, eligibility depends on how you use the Service:

Only state-licensed and/or locally licensed contractors may post or list their own jobs/projects through the Service, and only while the contractor’s license is active and in good standing with the applicable government agency, board, or authority that regulates and maintains contractor compliance in the relevant jurisdiction. You represent and warrant that any licensing information you provide is accurate and current, and you agree to promptly update it if it changes.

subcontractors, tradespeople, and other service providers may be eligible to unlock and access jobs/leads in a given jurisdiction if they hold the required authorization for that municipality (for example, an applicable occupational license, business tax receipt, registration, or other local qualification), and if that authorization is valid/active at the time of access.

TigerLeads may (but is not required to) request documentation or perform checks to verify licensing/registration status. You remain solely responsible for ensuring you are properly authorized to perform and solicit work in each jurisdiction where you use the Service. If your status becomes inactive, expires, is suspended, or is otherwise not in good standing, you must stop posting/unlocking within affected jurisdictions. TigerLeads may restrict access, remove postings, or suspend/terminate accounts for suspected misrepresentation or noncompliance.

TigerLeads provides a software platform that helps Customers identify and receive construction- related opportunities, including potential leads and project insights that may be derived from public records (such as permit filings, inspections, and related governmental data), third-party sources, and Customer-provided inputs.

TigerLeads is not a contractor, subcontractor, supplier, broker, agent, or referral service, and TigerLeads is not a party to any agreement between Customers and their prospects. You are solely responsible for evaluating leads, verifying information, and deciding whether and how to pursue any opportunity.

You are responsible for maintaining the confidentiality of your credentials and for all activity under your account. You agree to provide accurate information and keep it updated. You must promptly notify us of any unauthorized use or security breach involving your account.

Certain features require a paid subscription (“Subscription”). Your plan features, pricing, billing frequency, limits, and any usage-based charges may be described in the Service, a checkout flow, invoice, or order form (collectively, “Order Terms”). If Order Terms conflict with these Terms, the Order Terms control for that conflict.

You authorize TigerLeads (and our payment processors) to charge your payment method for recurring subscription fees and any applicable usage-based charges you incur. Fees are exclusive of taxes unless stated otherwise. You are responsible for all applicable taxes, duties, and similar government assessments (excluding taxes on our net income).
Unless your Order Terms say otherwise, Subscriptions automatically renew for the same term at the then-current rate unless canceled before renewal.

You may cancel your Subscription through your account settings (if available) or by contacting support at support@tigerleads.ai . Unless stated otherwise in your Order Terms, cancellation takes effect at the end of your current billing period, and you will retain access until then.
Unless required by law or explicitly stated in your Order Terms, all fees are non-refundable, and we do not provide refunds or credits for partial periods, unused access, or unused Credits.
TigerLeads may offer a credit system (“Credits”) that can be used to access certain lead deliveries, features, or usage-based components of the Service.
Credits:
have no cash value;
are non-transferable;
may not be resold, assigned, or sublicensed; and
may only be used within the Service as permitted by your Subscription.

Credits roll over while your Subscription remains active and in good standing (i.e., not canceled, expired, or terminated, and not delinquent on payment), unless your Order Terms specify a maximum cap or other limitation.

If your Subscription expires, is canceled, is deleted, or is terminated and you do not renew, then all remaining Credits will expire and be forfeited 30 days after the end of your last active Subscription period. If you renew/reactivate within that 30-day window, TigerLeads may (but is not required to) restore remaining Credits, unless prohibited by your plan rules or Order Terms.
We may suspend, revoke, or void Credits if we reasonably believe they were obtained or used through fraud, abuse, or violation of these Terms.
Leads and insights may be delivered through the dashboard, exports, email notifications, webhooks, or other methods supported by the Service.
Unless expressly stated in writing in your Order Terms, leads are non-exclusive and may be provided to multiple Customers.
TigerLeads does not guarantee:
that any lead is reachable, accurate, complete, current, or actionable;
that any permit will proceed to construction;
any minimum number of leads;
or any revenue or business outcomes.
You agree not to, and not to permit others to:
Use the Service for unlawful, harmful, fraudulent, or deceptive purposes.

Do-Not-Contact Handling Policy. The TigerLeads.ai Do-Not-Contact Handling Policy is incorporated into these Terms by reference as Addendum A. If you receive a do-not-contact request from a prospect, you agree to honor it and to record it within the Service (if available) or submit it to

Credits expire as described in Section 5; and
sections that should survive (including payment obligations, IP, disclaimers, limitation of liability indemnification, and dispute resolution) will survive.

This Do-Not-Contact Handling Policy (the “DNC Policy”) explains how Customers should handle “do-not-contact” requests received from prospects and how TigerLeads.ai processes DNC signals within the Service. This DNC Policy is incorporated into the Terms of Service by reference.

You are solely responsible for complying with applicable laws and rules governing outreach, including (where applicable) the TCPA, state telemarketing laws, CAN-SPAM, and other privacy/consumer protection requirements. This includes obtaining any required consents and honoring opt-out or do-not-contact requests.
TigerLeads.ai does not provide legal advice and does not guarantee that any outreach method is lawful.
A “Do-Not-Contact” request may include:
“Stop calling/texting/emailing me,” “unsubscribe,” “remove me,” or similar wording;
written requests (email, form submission, letter);
verbal requests communicated by phone; or
opt-out mechanisms offered by your outreach tools
If a prospect requests not to be contacted, you should treat that request as applying to th channel used and, as a best practice, all channels unless the prospect states otherwise.
Matching Limitations: suppression depends on matching identifiers (like phone/email). If future public records contain different identifiers, suppression may not catch them.
Project vs. Person: some leads reference a project/property rather than a specific individual. A different contact later associated with the same project may not be suppressed.
Maintain Your Own DNC Lists: TigerLeads’ DNC tools are convenience features. You should maintain and honor your own internal DNC lists across your CRM and outreach systems.
Timing: suppression may not be instantaneous. You remain responsible for ensuring you do not contact someone after receiving a DNC request.

If you send marketing emails, you must include required unsubscribe mechanisms (where applicable), process unsubscribe requests promptly, and avoid further marketing emails to unsubscribed recipients (except as permitted for transactional messages).
If you send texts, you should honor common opt-out keywords like “STOP” and ensure your texting system processes opt-outs properly.

You may not use, upload/mark data you do not have the right to use or to violate laws/rights. TigerLeads may suspend or terminate accounts that misuse the Service or repeatedly fail to honor DNC requests, as permitted by the Terms.
Customer Support: support@tigerleads.ai

h1: Helping Contractors & Suppliers Win More Work
h5: Tigerleads AI is a lead intelligence platform built specifically for contractors and suppliers across the United States. We use AI to track, analyze, and surface real project opportunities — so you can spend less time searching and more time closing.
From general contractors and subcontractors to material suppliers and specialty trades, we help businesses discover the right projects at the right time.
We aggregate activity data across a wide range of contractor and supplier industries, with continuous updates across major regions

We aggregate and analyze construction activity data across federal, state, and private sectors — continuously monitoring permits, bids, project announcements, and procurement updates across major US regions.
Our AI filters noise and delivers qualified, relevant leads tailored to your trade, location, and project size preferences.
No cold guessing. No wasted outreach. Just high-intent opportunities.
We believe in clarity — in pricing, in data sources, and in how our AI works. You always know where opportunities come from and why they’re relevant to you.
Our platform is designed to deliver consistent, up-to-date project data you can depend on. When opportunities move, you’ll know.
Construction is complex. Finding leads shouldn’t be. We turn scattered information into a clean, actionable workflow.
We build alongside our users. Every feature is shaped by feedback from contractors and suppliers actively bidding across the US.

This Privacy Policy explains how Alliance Data Solutions LLC, a Florida limited liability company doing business as TigerLeads.ai (“TigerLeads,” “we,” “us,” or “our”), collects, uses, discloses, and protects information when you use our websites, applications, dashboards, and related services (the “Service”. The Service is designed primarily for business users (contractors, suppliers, and related professionals). The Service may include information sourced from public records, including construction permits and related filings.
This Policy covers information we collect:
from you and your organization when you create an account, subscribe, configure filters, submit content, or contact us;
automatically through your use of the Service (device and usage data);
from public sources and third parties used to support the Service (including government/public records).
This Policy does not cover third-party websites or services you may access via links or integrations; those are governed by their own policies.
We may collect:
Account and profile information (name, company name, role, email, phone, address/service area login credentials).
Subscription and billing information (billing contact details; payment confirmation and limited payment metadata from our processors—typically we do not store full card numbers).
Customer Content (filters, notes, tags, uploads, lead dispositions, suppression preferences, and similar account data you provide).
Communications (messages to support@tigerleads.ai , sales communications, surveys, and feedback).
When you use the Service, we may collect:
Usage data (pages/features used, searches/filters, lead views, clicks, timestamps, referral URLs).
Device and technical data (IP address, browser type, device identifiers, OS, language, approximate location derived from IP).
Cookies and similar technologies (see Section 7).
To provide the Service, we may collect and process information from:
Public/government records (e.g., permit applications, permit status updates, inspection events, project addresses, applicant/owner/contractor names when publicly available).
Third-party data providers (where permitted) that help us improve coverage, deduplicate records, or enrich data.
Public records may contain personal information (for example, a property owner name or mailing address). Availability varies by jurisdiction, and records may change over time.
If you connect third-party tools (e.g., a CRM), we may receive information you authorize those tools to share with us. The scope depends on the integration and your settings.
We use information to:
Provide and operate the Service, including delivering leads/insights based on your settings.
Process subscriptions and payments, manage Credits, prevent fraud, and enforce billing terms.
Improve and maintain the Service, including troubleshooting, analytics, deduplication, and product development.
Secure the Service, including monitoring, preventing abuse, and enforcing our Terms of Service.
Communicate with you, including account notices, service announcements, and support responses.
Market the Service (subject to your choices), including sending product updates and offers.
Comply with legal obligations and respond to lawful requests.
We may disclose information in the following ways:

We use vendors (e.g., hosting, analytics, email delivery, support tools, payment processors). They may process information on our behalf under contractual obligations to protect it and use it only for providing services to us.
A core function of the Service is providing Customers with lead and project information. This may include information from public records and related insights necessary to use the Service.
If you enable integrations (e.g., exporting into a CRM), we may disclose information to those third parties at your direction.
We may disclose information if we believe in good faith it is necessary to:
comply with law, regulation, subpoena, or court order;
protect rights, safety, and security of TigerLeads, our users, or others;
investigate fraud, misuse, or security incidents.
If we undergo a merger, acquisition, financing, reorganization, or sale of assets, information may be transferred as part of that transaction (subject to reasonable safeguards).

We may create aggregated or de-identified data (for example, trend analyses by county or permit type). We may use and share this data for analytics, benchmarking, product improvement, and reporting, consistent with applicable law.
You can update certain account information through the Service or by contacting support.

You can opt out of marketing emails by using the unsubscribe link in those emails or contacting us. Even if you opt out, we may still send transactional or service messages (billing, security, outages, policy updates).
If you are an individual whose information appears in public records that we process, you may request that we:
confirm whether we process certain information about you (where feasible), and/or
suppress your information from being displayed in certain contexts (where feasible and permitted).

Because we rely on public records and must maintain data integrity, we may not be able to delete or alter underlying public-record facts, but we will consider reasonable suppression requests consistent with the Service and applicable law.
Submit requests to support@tigerleads.ai with sufficient detail to locate the relevant record(s).
Depending on your state of residence, you may have rights such as access, deletion (with exceptions), correction, and opt-out of certain disclosures deemed “sale” or “sharing” under some state laws.
To exercise these rights, email support@tigerleads.ai with “Privacy Request” in the subject line. We may verify your request and require additional information to confirm identity and prevent fraud.

You can manage your cookie preferences at any time by clicking the 'Cookie Settings' link in our footer. We use Osano as our cookie consent management platform — your choices will be remembered across sessions and pages.
We retain information for as long as necessary to:
provide the Service,
comply with legal obligations,
resolve disputes,
enforce our agreements,
maintain security and operational integrity.
Retention periods vary by data type and business need.

We use administrative, technical, and physical safeguards designed to protect information. No system is perfectly secure; you are responsible for maintaining strong passwords and controlling access to your account.

The Service is operated from the United States. If you access the Service from outside the U.S., you understand your information may be processed in the U.S. and other countries where our vendors operate.
The Service is not intended for children under the age 18. We do not knowingly collect personal information from children.

We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. We will post the updated version with a new effective date. Continued use of the Service after the effective date means you accept the updated Policy.

We stopped chasing bad leads and started closing real projects.
Daniel K., Remodeling Contractor
UrbanCraft Remodels · Denver, CO
Our support team is here to help. Send us an email and we will get back to you as soon as possible.
How does the credit system work?

Each job card displays a basic summary for free; if you find a project that fits your expertise, you spend a set number of credits to "unlock" it. This reveals the full project scope and the customer's direct contact information so you can reach out immediately.
What information do I get when I unlock a job?
Once a job is unlocked, you get everything you need to secure the contact. This includes the customer’s full name, phone number, and email address, as well as detailed project specifications
How are jobs verified before being posted?

We prioritize quality over quantity. Every lead on our dashboard undergoes an automated and manual vetting process to ensure the customer is high-intent and the project details are accurate. We filter out spam and incomplete requests so you only spend your credits on legitimate opportunities.
Can I post my own jobs on the platform?

While our primary focus is helping contractors and suppliers find work, you can certainly post projects if you need to subcontract a portion of a job or find a specific specialist. Simply head to the "Post a Job" section in your dashboard to list your requirements for our network.
Do credits expire?

No, credits do not expire. Monthly subscription credits typically reset with your billing cycle to encourage active lead-finding. However, any "Bonus Credits" or "Boost Pack" credits earned through first-time subscriptions or special promotions stay in your account as long as your subscription remains active.
How do I manage my subscription?

You have full control over your plan through your Account Settings. From there, you can upgrade your tier to access more monthly credits and seats, view your billing history, or update your payment methods via our secure Stripe integration.
How many credits does an unlock cost?
Unlock costs vary by opportunity type and value. You’ll always see the unlock cost before spending credits.
Are opportunities exclusive?

Opportunities may be visible to multiple users depending on plan and availability. TigerLeads is designed to help you act early—speed matters more than exclusivity.  Each Lead is available to 5 Unique User Types per category.  Once the 5 spaces are filled the Lead is no longer available for those Unique User Types.
Can my team use one account?
Yes—team plans support multiple seats so estimators, sales, and ops can work from the same pipeline.
What if I need help onboarding?
You can contact support or book a quick walkthrough—especially if you want help setting up trades, territory, and saved searches.
Join thousands of contractors and suppliers who are finding better opportunities every day. Start your free trial today—no credit card required.
